comments.summary: Word-count inconsistency: main.tex declares 2,310 body words; cover-letter.md declares 2,281. Re-run texcount once and propagate a single number to both files before submission.
references.bib retains verification-pending notes for lintom2026 and linehs2024; operator-step at submission release.

JOURNAL.md Generative-AI disclosure section now embeds an interpretive sentence about bilateral lifting; ensure this working-interpretation prose does not bleed into Editorial Manager submission metadata.

new_comments.summary: Hyphenation/tilde harmonisation across manuscript, cover letter and disclosure documents (Layer~1 vs Layer 1; AI-vs-operator). Editorial copy-edit pass before submission.

Section 4 paragraph 2 mentions 'a clinical-genomics journal of its own choice' without naming the journal the Layer-1 pipeline selected. Replace with the actual journal name plus a citation to the case-study manuscript or its preprint.

docs/ai-usage-disclosure.md correctly lists an operator-in-the-loop gate at commit acceptance and tag push; Section 4 implies the only human step is Layer 3. Add one clause acknowledging the operator-gated release tag so the manuscript matches the disclosure document.

acceptance_conditions_for_round3: At release tag, the Layer-3 conditional sentence at manuscript/main.tex line 144 must be collapsed to the actual branch (positive or null) with the real Delta C value and bootstrap CI substituted for the placeholders hat-delta, L, U. This is the C3 close-out gate.
N1, N2, N3 addressed in a final copy-edit pass.

external_check.findings: Hematology & Medical Oncology Fellow at Koo Foundation Sun Yat-Sen Cancer Center since 2023-08. Board-certified Internal Medicine (TSIM) and Medical Oncology (Taiwan Oncology Society). Four-paper record: HFNO in cancer patients, G-CSF timing in CIN, HSCT in T-cell lymphoma, theory-of-mind in LLM poker agents. Manuscript wording 'haematology and medical oncology fellow whose published clinical work is in hematologic malignancies and supportive care' is calibrated to the CV.

round_1_comments_open.summary: Failure-preservation carve-out for intentionally-overwritable artefacts (cohort-selection.json 'will be overwritten on the next pipeline run'); deferred by operator to next docs/design.md update; not surfaced in manuscript Limitations.

new_comments_round_2.summary: docs/disclosure2-schema.json does not constrain toolEnvelope keys to be a subset of models[].name; a producer could declare two models and a tool envelope keyed by neither, and the schema would accept it. Add a description or validation rule coupling the two.

docs/ledger.md lags HEAD by two commits (committed at 0810331, current HEAD 5fde223). Regeneration is deterministic (verified via two consecutive `uv run python scripts/regenerate_ledger.py` runs producing byte-identical output). Operator should wire the regeneration into the release-tag Makefile target so the tagged release ships a current ledger.

Manuscript Limitations now names four of the seven Round 1 residual failure modes (procedural enforcement, audit-subagent hallucination, model deprecation, reviewer-loop non-determinism). Reviewer-AI collusion and selective failure preservation across runs that were never committed are not surfaced; one Limitations sentence would close this.

verification_notes: Ran `uv run python scripts/regenerate_ledger.py` twice on the working tree at HEAD `5fde223`. Both runs produced byte-identical output (verified by diff -u, exit 0). Output: 25 commits, 0 tags.

Verified `case-study/analysis/99_reexec_check.py` is absent from the working tree (find returns no hits). The manuscript Section 5 acknowledges this and downgrades the distributional-reproducibility claim conditional on the script's absence — the correct honesty move.
Verified `docs/disclosure2-schema.json` exists (160 lines, JSON Schema draft 2020-12) and covers all six manifest items plus optional audit/reviewerSubagents/preregistration/honestyContract blocks.
Verified `docs/ledger.md` exists (45 lines, 23 commits at the snapshot commit `0810331`).
Verified `scripts/regenerate_ledger.py` exists (152 lines), uses git log --reverse, classifies by path prefix, has no time-of-day inputs, and is deterministic for a given commit range.
Verified Zhao et al. 2026 citation is present in references.bib as `zhaoHallucinations2026` and cited in main.tex at sections 1 and 2 with the 146,932-hallucination figure.

Verified manuscript Section 3 item 5 splits the audit log into 5a (re-execution), 5b (citation veracity including in-text claim-to-source alignment plus DOI plus author list plus journal plus year), and 5c (headline-statistic rederivation), with the explicit clause 'Partial discharge of (5a) alone does not satisfy item 5'.
Verified manuscript Section 5 declares the Layer-2 seal as testimonial rather than cryptographic and names `reviewer-logs/audit/session-launch.json` as the minimum operational evidence.

Verified manuscript Limitations names: testimonial three-layer separation, branch-protection-only enforcement of no-history-rewriting, reviewer-loop non-determinism, model-deprecation graceful degradation, operator prior-knowledge in Layer-3 thresholds.

Verified `git log --oneline` shows 25 commits; the most recent is `5fde223 reviewer-logs: add Round 1 response-to-reviewers cross-reference table`; the v0.4 manuscript revision commit is `da4ddce manuscript: v0.4 — full revision addressing 44 Round 1 reviewer comments`.

acceptance_criteria_assessment.distributional_reproducibility_notes: Section 5 specifies n=3 runs, concordance on selected sub-claim and statistical method, and headline effect size within original bootstrap 95% CI. Downgrade path is explicit when the implementing script is not shipped.
acceptance_criteria_assessment.layer_separation_notes: Section 5 names the seal as 'testimonial rather than cryptographic' with session-launch attestation file path; Limitations restates that the separation is procedural and testimonial.
acceptance_criteria_assessment.manifest_residual_blind_spots_notes: Four of the seven Round 1 blind spots are in Limitations; reviewer-AI collusion and uncommitted-run selective preservation are not. Sufficient for minor-revision; an additional sentence would clear `accept`.

reviewers.scope_verdict: In scope for Lancet Digital Health; would now send out for external peer review conditional on four operator-step items at submission. Zero blocking issues survive at the manuscript level.

new_comments_round_2.summary: Title-page word count says 2310; cover letter and texcount say 2281. Correct title page to 2281 (the Lancet IFA body-word definition excludes Key Messages box and front-matter declarations).

April 2026 Lancet DH editorial 'The rise of the agentic AI colleague' was surfaced via WebSearch snippet in Round 1 but full text was 403-gated; v0.4 §1 covers it implicitly under 'Both deployment-side editorials'. Recommended operator-level re-search; if the editorial exists, add one in-text citation alongside zouAgentic2025; if it does not, no edit needed.

Acknowledgements paragraph at 181 words is dense; consider a single paragraph break between the human-responsibility sentence and the limitations-encountered sentence to aid copy-edit scan. Pure suggestion, not required.

operator_step_items_for_submission_day: Fill cover-letter date, medRxiv DOI, Zenodo DOI, four reviewer names with affiliation and ORCID
Re-verify live Lancet Digital Health IFA ceiling on submission day (live PDF returned HTTP 403 again on 2026-05-21; ceiling re-verification has been deferred to operator for three consecutive checks)
Confirm or remove the April 2026 Lancet DH editorial citation (N2)
Correct title-page body word count from 2310 to 2281 (N1)

live_ifa_verification.risk: If live PDF shows 1500/20 ceiling (matching Lancet main title Viewpoint format) rather than the operator-asserted 2500/30, a 30% structural cut is required.

novelty_assessment.april_2026_handling: Implicit under 'Both deployment-side editorials' sentence in Section 1 paragraph 1; explicit citation recommended pending operator re-search
